title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Compiler Case: Default Exchange Coupling
summary: A task service publishes through the default exchange without naming the coupling, assumes one queue broadcasts, uses auto-ack workers, and calls round-robin dispatch fair despite unequal task cost. Planned artifact: Add and verify the default exchange coupling increment in direct-routing work distribution service. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Default Exchange Coupling · Theory Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6005 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Default Exchange Coupling contract for the supplied data.
